Ziqi Kang

University of Helsinki
Färkkilä Lab

SPACEstat: quantitative spatial point pattern and network analysis of the tumor microenvironment

Ziqi Kang

University of Helsinki
Färkkilä Lab

SPACEstat: quantitative spatial point pattern and network analysis of the tumor microenvironment

Background

Tumor microenvironment (TME) is a complex ecosystem. 
Improved methods to quantify the spatial TME are need to understand the tumor ecosystem.

2

Quantification of Physical TME

Speaker notes:
Tumor microenvironment is a complex ecosystem with clinical features, physical TME and functional TME. In order to better understand this complexity, we need to be able to quantify the spatial relationships and structures within the physical TME.

Background

Tumor microenvironment (TME) is a complex ecosystem. 
Improved methods to quantify the spatial TME are need to understand the tumor ecosystem.

2

Quantification of Physical TME

Speaker notes:
Tumor microenvironment is a complex ecosystem with clinical features, physical TME and functional TME. In order to better understand this complexity, we need to be able to quantify the spatial relationships and structures within the physical TME.

Background

Aims 
Quantifying spatial arrangement of single cells and their relationships to tissue compartments

3

Speaker notes:

Overview of SPACEstat workflow

A toolkit allows quantitative assessment at single-cell level or per-slide, focusing on spatial metrics

5

Speaker notes:
We describe the SPACEstat workflow in 3 steps. 
 The input is data frame in csv or Anndata with minimum features of X, Y and cell type labels which can be derived from spatial proteomics or transcriptomics.

Overview of SPACEstat workflow

A toolkit allows quantitative assessment at single-cell level or per-slide, focusing on spatial metrics

9

Speaker notes:
First, I will introduce how SPACEstat can quantify the spatial distributions at the single-cell level.

Calculate the cell-cell nearest neighbour distance distribution, provide median distance and AUC

Calculate the basic spatial features per cell type (e.g. Cluster density, Gini Density, etc)

10

SPACEstat quantifies spatial distribution at single-cell level

Speaker notes:
To understand the spatial TME; we first need to quantify the cellular distance distributions. 
This step does not require extra input from users, SPACEstat can calculate the cell-cell nearest neighbour distance distribution and provide median distance

Calculate the cell-cell nearest neighbour distance distribution, provide median distance and AUC

Calculate the basic spatial features per cell type (e.g. Cluster density, Gini Density, etc)

10

SPACEstat quantifies spatial distribution at single-cell level

Speaker notes:
To understand the spatial TME; we first need to quantify the cellular distance distributions. 
This step does not require extra input from users, SPACEstat can calculate the cell-cell nearest neighbour distance distribution and provide median distance

11

SPACEstat quantifies spatial distribution at single-cell level

Calculate the cell-cell nearest neighbour distance distribution, provide median distance and AUC

Calculate the basic spatial features per cell type (e.g. Cluster density, Gini Density, etc)

Speaker notes:
… and shown in a heatmap. Importantly, with SPACEstat you can quantify the basic spatial features of each cell type to better understand the conditions of the slide before you process with more detailed analysis [motivations!]. 

SPACEstat can go through each cell type and calculate the features such as cluster density or Gini density, which can describe how the cells distributed in the tissue.

11

SPACEstat quantifies spatial distribution at single-cell level

Speaker notes:
… and shown in a heatmap. Importantly, with SPACEstat you can quantify the basic spatial features of each cell type to better understand the conditions of the slide before you process with more detailed analysis [motivations!]. 

SPACEstat can go through each cell type and calculate the features such as cluster density or Gini density, which can describe how the cells distributed in the tissue.

12

User can define: 
Cell types of interest, e.g. immune cells
Region compartments of interest, e.g. tumor & stroma
Threshold for cell type ratios to determine the compartment labels

SPACEstat quantifies spatial distribution at single-cell level

Speaker notes:
Next, with SPACEstat you can quantify cellular compartment infiltration. 

Users can define the cell types and the type of compartments they want to study, also the threshold for context determination.

SPACEstat quantifies spatial distribution at single-cell level

Speaker notes:
Next, with SPACEstat you can quantify cellular compartment infiltration. 

Users can define the cell types and the type of compartments they want to study, also the threshold for context determination.

immune infiltration index (I3) =
immune_density_tumor / immune_density_stroma

13

SPACEstat quantifies spatial distribution at single-cell level

User can define: 
Cell types of interest, e.g. immune cells
Region compartments of interest, e.g. tumor & stroma
Threshold for cell type ratios to determine the compartment labels

Speaker notes:
Here on the slide immune cells were used as an example. 

Based on the cellular compartment labels, the users can calculate the immune infiltration index by dividing the density of immune cells in 2 tissue compartments.

immune infiltration index (I3) =
immune_density_tumor / immune_density_stroma

SPACEstat quantifies spatial distribution at single-cell level

User can identify the interface between any 2 groups of cells
User can define the cell type of interest and 
Calculate the distance between cell <—> interface (global or per-side).

Speaker notes:
Interaction between compartments creates interfaces. 

To enable quantification of the spatial relationships to the tissue interfaces, Users can specify the interface between 2 groups of the cells, depends on the research questions.

After interface identification, users can define a list of cell types of interest and calculated the distance from cell to the interface, globally (if you don’t do the previous step) or per compartment.

Findings - 1 with SPACEstat: immune aggregation on TSI in ovarian tumor ATRi therapy responder & non-responder

16

PD1+ CD8 T cells were significantly closer to the stromal side of the TSI in the responders compared to the non-responders

Dataset: 
12 whole slide Immunofluorescence images; 
6 Responders vs 6 non-responders.

Unpublished result, please do not post

Speaker notes:
I’m showing here a biological discovery that can be made based on the spatial quantification on single-cell level. 
We used a dataset of 12 whole slide images from ovarian cancer. 

We found the T cells and PD1+ CD8 T cells were located significantly closer to the stromal side of the TSI in the tumors that responded to ATRi -a targeted therapeutic-, in comparison to the non-responders.

Findings - 1 with SPACEstat: immune aggregation on TSI in ovarian tumor ATRi therapy responder & non-responder

PD1+ CD8 T cells were significantly closer to the stromal side of the TSI in the responders compared to the non-responders

Speaker notes:
I’m showing here a biological discovery that can be made based on the spatial quantification on single-cell level. 
We used a dataset of 12 whole slide images from ovarian cancer. 

We found the T cells and PD1+ CD8 T cells were located significantly closer to the stromal side of the TSI in the tumors that responded to ATRi -a targeted therapeutic-, in comparison to the non-responders.

SPACEstat enables cellular network and spatial pattern analysis

User can choose 1 or multiple cell types to generate the network
Record the feature of the networks.

Set threshold of longest edge

Speaker notes:
… record the features of the networks such as the total number of components and the size largest components. 

In the package, there will also be a data object to store the network components with the X, Y coordinates. 

The only parameter to tune is the longest edge allowed when forming the network, we will provide functions for user to diagnose and choose a proper threshold.

Event: Interaction between 2 cellular networks (e.g. CD4T & CD8T)
We can study how does this event distribute across the space (clustering? Dispersed?)

24

SPACEstat enables cellular network and spatial pattern analysis

Speaker notes:
Consider the interaction of 2 network components as an event. 

Across the whole image you are studying, there are many such events happening. 
How can you describe their behavior per slide? To quantify it, you need this step “spatial pattern analysis”.

Findings - 2: Immune hub organisation can be used to stratify samples

26

79 whole slide Immunofluorescence images from ovarian cancer patients
17 markers, 10 cell types
2 subtypes (HRD & HRP)

Unpublished result, please do not post

PanCK
aSMA
CD20
CD8a

HRP

HRD

Speaker notes:
To be able to better understand a heterogenicity of cell interactions, we can cluster the immune hub spatial patterns across the dataset. 

We used a dataset including 79 metastatic ovarian cancer whole slide images to quantify the network structures in the TME. 
We build 2 networks for lymphonets and myelonets, and explore their spatial interactions.

We build 2 networks for lymphonets and myelonets, and explore their spatial interactions.

Findings - 2: Immune hub organisation can be used to stratify samples

27

Unpublished result, please do not post

Speaker notes:
In the heatmap, we identified 7 different clusters, each showing distinct spatial feature metrics. 

Notably, the samples differ in how strongly their immune hubs are spatially clustered across tissue scales. 

An example from cluster 0 show strong cluster across all spatial scales from 0-5000 µm. That means immune hubs are clustered in short distance and those clusters also connect over longer distances across the tissue

Findings - 2: Immune hub organisation can be used to stratify samples

27

Unpublished result, please do not post

Speaker notes:
In the heatmap, we identified 7 different clusters, each showing distinct spatial feature metrics. 

Notably, the samples differ in how strongly their immune hubs are spatially clustered across tissue scales. 

An example from cluster 0 show strong cluster across all spatial scales from 0-5000 µm. That means immune hubs are clustered in short distance and those clusters also connect over longer distances across the tissue

Findings - 2: Immune hub organisation can be used to stratify samples

28

Unpublished result, please do not post

Speaker notes:
. In another example from cluster 5, the immune hubs show clustering in short distances but then distributed randomly in long distances, indicating a lack of large scale structures.

SPACEstat is a user-friendly package with a visualization solution

Visualization - with Re-run

29

Speaker notes:
For user friendly application we integrated SPACEstat with scanpy and implemented with Re-run, to ensure users can visualize the spatial structures and networks

SPACEstat is a user-friendly package with a visualization solution

Visualization - with Re-run

29

Speaker notes:
For user friendly application we integrated SPACEstat with scanpy and implemented with Re-run, to ensure users can visualize the spatial structures and networks

Conclusions

SPACEstat standardises spatial measurements within each slide, enabling comparison at the single-cell level, and across samples.

SPACEstat uncovers functionally meaningful cell–cell and region-specific interactions. 

SPACEstat extends beyond cell–cell distances to quantify immune network topology and hub regularity.

Want to know more?
Please come to our poster: 195 :)

Conclusions

SPACEstat standardises spatial measurements within each slide, enabling comparison at the single-cell level, and across samples.

SPACEstat uncovers functionally meaningful cell–cell and region-specific interactions. 

SPACEstat extends beyond cell–cell distances to quantify immune network topology and hub regularity.

Want to know more?
Please come to our poster: 195 :)

Generalization & Visualization

To test whether SPACEstat generalizes beyond ovarian cancer, we will apply SPACEstat on 2 independent datasets from different platforms: MIBI-TOF in breast cancer and CODEX in colorectal cancer. 

Visualization - with Re-run
